titlestmt.author: Compiled by Sylvia A. Nyana and Brenda Marston

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-07-28 18:35:05 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Audiotapes, studio and meeting notes, correspondence, articles.
did.abstract: Audiotapes of "Being Ourselves," a program that was produced by the Ithaca Feminist Radio Collective and broadcast in Ithaca, NY, on Sunday mornings. Also, notes and files from collective meetings and relating to producing the show.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Audiotapes of "Being Ourselves," a program that was produced by the Ithaca Feminist Radio Collective and broadcast in Ithaca, NY, on Sunday mornings. Also, notes and files from collective meetings and relating to producing the show.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: The IFRC collection consists of six series, arranged with print material first, then the audio material, and lastly miscellany: I. General, II. Funding, III. Program Planning, IV. Printed Material, V. "Being Ourselves" Audiotapes, and VI. Miscellaneous

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ithaca Feminist Radio Collective, "Being Ourselves" Records, #6483. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Designed to address a broad spectrum of women.s issues, the radio show "Being Ourselves" aired interviews with local and visiting activists, writers, artists, and thinkers, as well as women.s music. Collective members changed over the years. An incomplete list of members includes: Leni Hochman, Judy Scherer, Jennifer Greene, Sara Shenk, Robin Whittlesey, Miriam Graham, Patrice Jennings, Deb Whiter, and Wendy Kohli.

did.unittitle: Contact sheet of photograph taken for March 26, 1987 Ithaca Times story
